methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $12M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Picogrid is a Seed-stage company in the Defense & Security sector, founded by Zane Mountcastle, having raised $12.3M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 10 comparable companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Tenna Systems, Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Saronic, and Flock Safety—across the same sector at similar or adjacent development stages.

Two triangulation methods are applied. Stage-Progression analysis uses the $12.3M raised as a proxy baseline and applies a typical 2.5x multiplier for Seed-to-next-stage advancement, implying a valuation of $31M. Peer-Median analysis examines four same-sector comparables: Tenna Systems at $60M (Seed stage), Ares Industries at $150M (Series A), Vatn Systems at $300M (Series A), and Stark Defence at $500M (Series A). The median across these four is $225M, with a range spanning $60M to $500M.

The two methods produce substantially divergent signals. Triangulation across both approaches yields a median valuation of $128M, with a low bound of $26M and a high bound of $259M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around whether Picogrid's technology maturity and market traction track nearer to Tenna Systems or to later-stage, higher-valued peers. Key uncertainties include actual customer adoption metrics, technology differentiation relative to incumbents, and the appropriate staging adjustment from Seed. A narrower range would require clarity on Picogrid's commercial progress relative to peer benchmarks.
